scenario: Northwind Bank uses Camunda 8.8 signal events to coordinate roughly 4000 events broadcast per day. A modeler must define a signal whose name is computed at runtime from a process variable awaitingAction so that intermediate catch events subscribe to the correct dynamic name during activation.
question: Which rule governs the signal name definition for an intermediate catch event in this case?

options.text: The signal name may be a FEEL expression evaluated when the catch event is activated and the evaluation must result in a string used as the subscription name
The signal name may be a FEEL expression evaluated when the process is deployed and the evaluation must result in a string used as the subscription name
The signal name must always be a static value and runtime variables can be supplied separately through a correlationKey expression on the catch event
The signal name may be a FEEL expression evaluated at activation and the evaluation must result in a list of strings so the catch event can subscribe to several names

a.text: Correct. A signal name can be defined as a static value or as a FEEL expression. For signal events other than signal start events, the expression is evaluated on activation of the event, and the result must be a string.

b.text: Incorrect. Deploy-time evaluation of the signal name applies only when the expression belongs to a signal start event of the process, not to intermediate catch events.

c.text: Incorrect. Signal events do not require a static name and do not use a correlationKey; the name itself can be a FEEL expression evaluated at activation that resolves to a string.

d.text: Incorrect. The signal name evaluation must result in a string, not a list of strings, so subscribing to several names through one signal name expression is not supported.

explanation: A signal references a signal element with a name. The name can be static or a FEEL expression; for signal start events it is evaluated on deployment, otherwise it is evaluated on activation of the signal event, and the result must be a string.
